title: Cumulative global production of plastics
subtitle: Cumulative annual production of polymer resin and fibers.
caption:
Cumulative global production of plastics

Cumulative annual production of polymer resin and fibers.

The area chart tracks the total accumulated global production of polymer resin and fibers from 1950 through 2019, measured in billions of tonnes. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 10 billion tonnes, while the horizontal axis marks decades from 1950 to 2019. The data shows a distinct exponential growth pattern, where the rate of accumulation increases significantly over time. From 1950 until approximately 1970, the cumulative total remains low, staying well below the 1 billion tonne mark. A visible inflection point occurs around 1980, where the curve begins to steepen, crossing the 2 billion tonne threshold near 1990. The growth accelerates further in the subsequent decades; the total reaches roughly 4 billion tonnes by the year 2000 and climbs to approximately 6 billion tonnes by 2010. The final segment of the chart, spanning 2010 to 2019, displays the steepest slope, with the cumulative volume rising from 6 billion tonnes to just under 10 billion tonnes by the end of the period. The visual representation highlights that the majority of all plastic ever produced was manufactured in the most recent decades shown, as the area expands rapidly toward the right side of the timeline. The curve ends at its highest point in 2019, indicating that the total accumulated mass of plastics continues to rise without plateauing within the observed timeframe.
